name: lkml

metadata.name: lkml
metadata.summary: A speedy LookML parser implemented in pure Python.

documentation:
[![CircleCI](https://img.shields.io/circleci/build/github/joshtemple/lkml.svg)](https://circleci.com/gh/joshtemple/lkml)
[![Codecov](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/joshtemple/lkml.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/joshtemple/lkml)
[![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black)
[![Read the Docs](https://img.shields.io/readthedocs/lkml)](https://lkml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)

# lkml

A speedy LookML parser and serializer implemented in pure Python.

**Read the documentation on [Read the Docs](https://lkml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).**

`lkml.load` parses LookML strings to Python objects or JSON strings. `lkml.dump` serializes (generates) LookML strings from Python objects.

Why should you use `lkml`?
- Tested on **over 160K lines of LookML** from public repositories on GitHub
- Parses a typical view or model file in < **10 ms** (excludes I/O time)
- Written in pure, modern Python 3.7 with **no external dependencies**
- A **full unit test suite** with excellent coverage

Interested in contributing to `lkml`? Check out the [contributor guidelines](CONTRIBUTING.md).

## How do I install it?

`lkml` is available to install on [pip](https://pypi.org/project/lkml/) via the following command:

```
pip install lkml
```

functions.name: lkml.utils.strip
documentation: Modified str.strip() which returns the stripped whitespace.

modules.name: lkml.keys
documentation: Defines constant sequences of LookML keys and helper methods.

functions.name: lkml.keys.pluralize
documentation: Converts a singular key like "explore" to a plural key, e.g. 'explores'.

functions.name: lkml.keys.singularize
documentation: Converts a plural key like "explores" to a singular key, e.g. 'explore'.

modules.name: lkml
documentation: A speedy LookML parser and serializer implemented in pure Python.

functions.name: lkml.parse
documentation:
Parse LookML into a parse tree.

Args:
    text: The LookML string to be parsed.

Returns:
    A document node, the root of the parse tree.

functions.name: lkml.load
documentation:
Parse LookML into a Python dictionary.

Args:
    stream: File object or string containing LookML to be parsed

Raises:
    TypeError: If stream is neither a string or a file object

functions.name: lkml.dump
documentation:
Serialize a Python dictionary into LookML.

Args:
    obj: The Python dictionary to be serialized to LookML
    file_object: An optional file object to save the LookML string to

Returns:
    A LookML string if no file_object is passed

functions.name: lkml.parse_args
documentation: Parse command-line arguments.

functions.name: lkml.cli
documentation: Command-line entry point for lkml.

modules.name: lkml.simple
documentation:
Interface classes between the parse tree and a data structure of primitives.

These classes facilitate parsing and generation to and from simple data structures like
lists and dictionaries, and allow users to parse and generate LookML without needing
to interact with the parse tree.

functions.name: lkml.simple.flatten
documentation: Flattens a singly-nested list of lists into a list of items.

classes.name: lkml.simple.DictVisitor

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictVisitor.update_tree
documentation:
Add one dictionary to an existing dictionary, handling certain repeated keys.

This method is primarily responsible for handling repeated keys in LookML like
`dimension` or `set`, which can exist more than once in LookML but cannot be
repeated in a Python dictionary.

This method checks the list of valid repeated keys and combines the values of
that key in `target` and/or `update` into a list and assigns a plural key (e.g.
`dimensions` instead of `dimension`).

Args:
    target: Existing dictionary of parsed LookML
    update: New dictionary to be added to target

Raises:
    KeyError: If `update` has more than one key
    KeyError: If the key in `update` already exists and would overwrite existing

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictVisitor.visit_container
documentation: Creates a dict from a container node by visiting its children.

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictVisitor.visit_block
documentation: Creates a dict from a block node by visiting its children.

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictVisitor.visit_list
documentation: Creates a dict from a list node by visiting its children.

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictVisitor.visit_pair
documentation: Creates a dict from pair node by visiting its type and value tokens.

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictVisitor.visit_token
documentation: Creates a string from a syntax token.

Creates a primitive representation of the parse tree.

Traverses the parse tree and transforms each node type into a dict. Each dict is
combined into one nested dict. Also handles the grouping of fields with plural keys
like `dimension` or `view` into lists.

Attributes:

: depth: Tracks the level of nesting.

classes.name: lkml.simple.DictParser

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.increase_level
documentation:
Increases the indent level of the current line by one tab.

This also resets the latest node, mainly for formatting reasons.

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.decrease_level
documentation: Decreases the indent level of the current line by one tab.

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.indent
documentation: Returns the level-adjusted indent.

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.newline_indent
documentation: Returns a newline plus the current indent.

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.prefix
documentation: Returns the currently appropriate, preceding whitespace.

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.is_plural_key
documentation:
Returns True if the key is a repeatable key.

For example, `dimension` can be repeated, but `sql` cannot be.

The key `allowed_value` is a special case and changes behavior depending on its
parent key. If its parent key is `access_grant`, it is a list and cannot be
repeated. Otherwise, it can be repeated.

The parent key `query` is also a special case, where children are kept as lists.
See issue #53.

Args:
    key: The name of the key to test.

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.resolve_filters
documentation:
Parse the key `filters` according to the context.

In LookML, the `filters` key is wildly inconsistent and can have three
different syntaxes. This method determines the syntax that should be used based
on the context and parses the appropriate node.

Args:

: values: The contents of the `filters` block. Provides context to resolve.

Returns:

: A block or list node depending on the resolution.

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.parse
documentation: Parses a primitive representation of LookML into a parse tree.

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.expand_list
documentation:
Expands and parses a list of values for a repeatable key.

Args:
    key: A repeatable LookML field type (e.g. "views" or "dimension_groups")
    values: A sequence of objects to be parsed

Returns:
    A list of block, list, or pair nodes, depending on the list's contents.

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.parse_any
documentation:
Dynamically serializes a Python object based on its type.

Args:
    key: A LookML field type (e.g. "suggestions" or "hidden")
    value: A string, tuple, or list to serialize

Raises:
    TypeError: If input value is not of a valid type

Returns:
    A generator of serialized string chunks

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.parse_block
documentation:
Serializes a dictionary to a LookML block.

Args:
    key: A LookML field type (e.g. "dimension")
    fields: A dictionary to serialize (e.g. {"sql": "${TABLE}.order_id"})
    name: An optional name of the block (e.g. "order_id")

Returns:
    A generator of serialized string chunks

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.parse_list
documentation:
Serializes a sequence to a LookML block.

Args:
    key: A LookML field type (e.g. "fields")
    values: A sequence to serialize (e.g. ["orders.order_id", "orders.item"])

Returns:
    A generator of serialized string chunks

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.parse_pair
documentation:
Serializes a key and value to a LookML pair.

Args:
    key: A LookML field type (e.g. "hidden")
    value: The value string (e.g. "yes")

Returns:
    A generator of serialized string chunks

methods.name: lkml.simple.DictParser.parse_token
documentation:
Parses a value into a token, quoting it if required by the key or forced.

Args:
    key: A LookML field type (e.g. "hidden")
    value: The value string (e.g. "yes")
    force_quote: True if value should always be quoted

Returns:
    A generator of serialized string chunks

Parses a Python dictionary into a parse tree.

Review the grammar specified for the Parser class to understand how LookML
is represented. The grammar details the differences between blocks, pairs, keys,
and values.

Attributes:
    parent_key: The name of the key at the previous level in a LookML block.
    level: The number of indentations appropriate for the current position.
    base_indent: Whitespace representing one tab.
    latest_node: The type of the last node to be parsed.

classes.name: lkml.visitors.BasicVisitor

methods.name: lkml.visitors.BasicVisitor._visit
documentation: For each node, visit its children.

Visitor class that calls the `_visit` method for every node type.

This class doesn't actually do anything when visiting a tree other than traverse
the nodes. It's meant to be used as a base class for building more useful and
complex visitors. For example, override any of the `visit_` methods for node-type
specific behavior.

classes.name: lkml.visitors.LookMlVisitor
documentation: Converts a parse tree into a string by casting every node.

classes.name: lkml.visitors.BasicTransformer

methods.name: lkml.visitors.BasicTransformer._visit_items
documentation: Visit a node whose children are held in the `items` attribute.

methods.name: lkml.visitors.BasicTransformer._visit_container
documentation: Visit a node whose only child is the `container` attribute.
Visitor class that returns a new tree, modifying the tree as needed.

modules.name: lkml.tree
documentation: Node and token classes that make up the parse tree.

functions.name: lkml.tree.items_to_str
documentation: Converts each item to a string and joins them together.

classes.name: lkml.tree.SyntaxToken

methods.name: lkml.tree.SyntaxToken.format_value
documentation: Returns the value itself, subclasses may modify the value first.

methods.name: lkml.tree.SyntaxToken.accept
documentation: Accepts a visitor and calls the visitor's token method on itself.

Stores a text value with optional prefix or suffix trivia.

For example, a syntax token might represent meaningful punctuation like a curly
brace or the type or value of a LookML field. A syntax token can also store trivia,
comments or whitespace that precede or follow the token value. The parser attempts
to assign these prefixes and suffixes intelligently to the corresponding tokens.

Attributes:
    value: The text represented by the token.
    prefix: Comments or whitespace preceding the token.
    suffix: Comments or whitespace following the token.

classes.name: lkml.tree.SyntaxNode

methods.name: lkml.tree.SyntaxNode.children
documentation: Returns all child SyntaxNodes, but not SyntaxTokens.

methods.name: lkml.tree.SyntaxNode.line_number
documentation: Returns the line number of the first SyntaxToken in the node

methods.name: lkml.tree.SyntaxNode.accept
documentation:
Accepts a Visitor that can interact with the node.

The visitor pattern allows for flexible algorithms that can traverse the tree
without needing to be defined as methods on the tree itself.
Abstract base class for members of the parse tree that have child nodes.

classes.name: lkml.tree.PairNode

methods.name: lkml.tree.PairNode.accept
documentation: Accepts a visitor and calls the visitor's pair method on itself.

A simple LookML field, e.g. `hidden: yes`.

Attributes:

: type: The field type, the value that precedes the colon.
  value: The field value, the value that follows the colon.
  colon: An optional Colon SyntaxToken. If not supplied, a default colon is

  > created with a single space suffix after the colon.

classes.name: lkml.tree.ListNode

methods.name: lkml.tree.ListNode.accept
documentation: Accepts a visitor and calls the visitor's list method on itself.

A LookML list, enclosed in square brackets. Like `fields` or `filters`.

Attributes:

: type: The field type, the value that precedes the colon.
  items: A tuple of pair nodes or syntax tokens, depending on the list style.
  left_bracket: A syntax token for the opening bracket "[".
  right_bracket: A syntax token for the closing bracket "]".
  colon: An optional Colon SyntaxToken. If not supplied, a default colon is

  > created with a single space suffix after the colon.

  trailing_comma: Include a trailing comma after the last item.

classes.name: lkml.tree.ContainerNode

methods.name: lkml.tree.ContainerNode.accept
documentation: Accepts a visitor and calls the visitor's container method on itself.

A sequence of nodes, either at the top level of a document, or within a block.

Attributes:
    items: A tuple of the contained nodes.
    top_level: If the container is the top level of the LookML document.

Raises:
    KeyError: If a key already exists in the tree and would be overwritten.

classes.name: lkml.tree.BlockNode

methods.name: lkml.tree.BlockNode.accept
documentation: Accepts a visitor and calls the visitor's block method on itself.

A LookML block, enclosed in curly braces. Like `view` or `dimension`.

Attributes:

: type: The field type, the value that precedes the colon.
  left_brace: A syntax token for the opening brace "{".
  right_brace: A syntax token for the closing brace "}".
  colon: An optional Colon SyntaxToken. If not supplied, a default colon is

  > created with a single space suffix after the colon.

  name: An optional name token, the value that follows the colon.
  container: A container node that holds the block's child nodes.

classes.name: lkml.tree.DocumentNode

methods.name: lkml.tree.DocumentNode.accept
documentation: Accepts a visitor and calls the visitor's visit method on itself.

The root node of the parse tree.

Attributes:
    container: The top-level container node.
    prefix: Leading whitespace or comments before the document.
    suffix: Trailing whitespace or comments after the document.

classes.name: lkml.tree.Visitor
documentation: Abstract base class for visitors that interact with the parse tree.

modules.name: lkml.tokens
documentation: Tokens used by the lexer to tokenize LookML.

classes.name: lkml.tokens.Token

methods.name: lkml.tokens.Token.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a Token.

Args:
    line_number: The corresponding line in the text where this token begins

methods.name: lkml.tokens.Token.__eq__
documentation: Compare one Token to another by their type.

methods.name: lkml.tokens.Token.__repr__
documentation:
Returns the token's string representation, truncated to 25 characters.

If the token has a `value` attribute, include that in the output.

Examples:
    >>> token = Token(1)
    >>> token.__repr__()
    'Token()'

    >>> token.value = 'A string value'
    >>> token.__repr__()
    'Token(A string value)'

    >>> token.value = 'A very, very, very long string value'
    >>> token.__repr__()
    'Token(A very, very, very long s ... )'
Base class for LookML tokens, lexed from LookML strings.

classes.name: lkml.tokens.ContentToken

methods.name: lkml.tokens.ContentToken.__init__
documentation:

Args:

methods.name: lkml.tokens.ContentToken.__eq__
documentation: Compare one ContentToken to another by their values.
Base class for LookML tokens that contain a string of content.

modules.name: lkml.lexer
documentation: Splits a LookML string into a sequence of tokens.

classes.name: lkml.lexer.Lexer

methods.name: lkml.lexer.Lexer.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the Lexer with a LookML string and sets the index.

Args:
    text: LookML string to be lexed

methods.name: lkml.lexer.Lexer.peek
documentation: Returns the character at the current index of the text being lexed.

methods.name: lkml.lexer.Lexer.peek_multiple
documentation:
Returns the next n characters from the current index in the text being lexed.

Args:
    length: The number of characters to return

methods.name: lkml.lexer.Lexer.advance
documentation:
Moves the index forward by n characters.

Args:
    length: The number of positions forward to move the index.

methods.name: lkml.lexer.Lexer.consume
documentation: Returns the current index character and advances the index 1 character.

methods.name: lkml.lexer.Lexer.scan
documentation:
Tokenizes LookML into a sequence of tokens.

This method skips through the text being lexed until it finds a character that
indicates the start of a new token. It consumes the relevant characters and adds
the tokens to a sequence until it reaches the end of the text.

methods.name: lkml.lexer.Lexer.check_for_expression_block
documentation: Returns True if the input string is an expression block.

methods.name: lkml.lexer.Lexer.scan_whitespace
documentation:
Returns a token from one or more whitespace characters.

        Example:
            >>> lexer = Lexer("

         Hello")
            >>> lexer.scan_whitespace()
            LinebreakToken('

', 1)

methods.name: lkml.lexer.Lexer.scan_comment
documentation:
Returns a token from a comment.

The initial pound (#) character is consumed in the scan method, so this
method only scans for a newline or end of file to indicate the end of the token.

The pound character is added back to the beginning to the token to emphasize
the importance of any leading whitespace that follows.

Example:
    >>> lexer = Lexer(" Disregard this line\n")
    >>> lexer.scan_comment()
    CommentToken(# Disregard this line)

methods.name: lkml.lexer.Lexer.scan_expression_block
documentation:
Returns an token from an expression block string.

This method strips any trailing whitespace from the expression string, since
Looker usually adds an extra space before the `;;` terminal.

Example:
    >>> lexer = Lexer("SELECT * FROM ${TABLE} ;;")
    >>> lexer.scan_expression_block()
    ExpressionBlockToken(SELECT * FROM ${TABLE})

methods.name: lkml.lexer.Lexer.scan_literal
documentation:
Returns a token from a literal string.

Example:
    >>> lexer = Lexer("yes")
    >>> lexer.scan_literal()
    LiteralToken(yes)

methods.name: lkml.lexer.Lexer.scan_quoted_literal
documentation:
Returns a token from a quoted literal string.

The initial double quote character is consumed in the scan method, so this
method only scans for the trailing quote to indicate the end of the token.

Example:
    >>> lexer = Lexer('Label"')
    >>> lexer.scan_quoted_literal()
    QuotedLiteralToken(Label)

Splits a LookML string into a sequence of tokens.

Attributes:
    text: Raw LookML to parse, padded with null character to denote end of stream
    index: Position of lexer in characters as it traverses the text
    tokens: Sequence of tokens that contain the relevant chunks of text
    line_number: Position of lexer in lines as it traverses the text

modules.name: lkml.parser
documentation: Parses a sequence of tokenized LookML into a parse tree.

functions.name: lkml.parser.backtrack_if_none
documentation:
Decorates parsing methods to backtrack to a previous position on failure.

This method sets a marker at the current position before attempting to run a
parsing method. If the parsing method fails and returns `None`, it resets the
index to the marker.

It also keeps track of the farthest index of progress in case all parsing
methods fail and we need to return a SyntaxError to the user with a character
number.

Args:
    fn (Callable): The method to be decorated for backtracking.

classes.name: lkml.parser.CommaSeparatedValues

methods.name: lkml.parser.CommaSeparatedValues.append
documentation: Add a value to the private _values list.

methods.name: lkml.parser.CommaSeparatedValues.values
documentation: Return the private _values list, cast to a tuple.
Helper class to store a series of values and a flag for a trailing comma.

classes.name: lkml.parser.Parser

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the Parser with a stream of tokens and sets the index.

Args:
    stream: Lexed sequence of tokens to be parsed

Raises:
    TypeError: If an object in the stream is not a valid token

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.jump_to_index
documentation: Sets the parser index to a specified value.

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.peek
documentation: Returns the token at the current index.

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.advance
documentation:
Moves the index forward by n characters.

Args:
    length: The number of positions forward to move the index.

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.consume
documentation: Returns the current index character and advances the index by 1 token.

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.consume_token_value
documentation: Returns the value of the current index token, advancing the index 1 token.

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.consume_trivia
documentation: Returns all continuous trivia values.

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.check
documentation:
Compares the current index token type to specified token types.

Args:
    *token_types: A variable number of token types to check against.
    skip_trivia: Ignore trivia tokens when searching for a match.

Raises:
    TypeError: If one or more of the token_types are not actual token types

Returns:
    True if the current token matches one of the token_types.

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.parse
documentation:
Main method of this class and a wrapper for the container parser.

Returns:
    A document node, the root node of the LookML parse tree.

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.parse_container
documentation:
Returns a container node that contains any number of children.

Grammar: `expression` ← `(block / pair / list)*`

Returns:

: A node with the parsed container or None if the grammar doesn't match.

Raises:

: SyntaxError: If unable to find a matching grammar rule for the stream

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.parse_block
documentation:
Returns a node that represents a LookML block.

Grammar: `block` ← `key literal? "{" expression "}"`

Returns:

: A node with the parsed block or None if the grammar doesn't match.

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.parse_pair
documentation:
Returns a dictionary that represents a LookML key/value pair.

Grammar: `pair` ← `key value`

Returns:

: A dictionary with the parsed pair or None if the grammar doesn't match.

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.parse_key
documentation:
Returns a syntax token that represents a literal key and colon character.

Grammar: `key` ← `literal ":"`

Returns:

: A tuple of syntax tokens with the parsed key and colon or None if the
  grammar doesn't match.

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.parse_value
documentation:
Returns a syntax token that represents a value.

Grammar: `value` ← `literal / quoted_literal / expression_block`

Returns:

: A syntax token with the parsed value or None if the grammar doesn't match.

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.parse_list
documentation:
Returns a node that represents a LookML list.

Grammar: `list` ← `key "[" csv? "]"`

Returns:

: A node with the parsed list or None if the grammar doesn't match

methods.name: lkml.parser.Parser.parse_csv
documentation:
Returns a CSV object that represents comma-separated LookML values.

Returns:

: A container with the parsed values or None if the grammar doesn't match

Grammar:

: `csv` ←
  `","? (literal / quoted_literal) ("," (literal / quoted_literal))* ","?`

Parses a sequence of tokenized LookML into a parse tree.

This parser is a recursive descent parser which uses the grammar listed below (in
PEG format). Each grammar rule aligns with a corresponding method (e.g.
parse_expression).

Grammar:
: - `expression` ← `(block / pair / list)*`
  - `block` ← `key literal? "{" expression "}"`
  - `pair` ← `key value`
  - `list` ← `key "[" csv? "]"`
  - `csv` ← `(literal / quoted_literal) ("," (literal / quoted_literal))* ","?`
  - `value` ← `literal / quoted_literal / expression_block`
  - `key` ← `literal ":"`
  - `expression_block` ← `[^;]* ";;"`
  - `quoted_literal` ← `'"' [^\"]+ '"'`
  - `literal` ← `[0-9A-Za-z_]+`

Attributes:

: tokens: A sequence of tokens to be parsed.
  index: The position in the token sequence being parsed.
  progress: The farthest index of progress during parsing.
  depth: The level of recursion into nested expressions.
  log_debug: A flag indicating that debug messages should be logged. This flag

  > exits to turn off logging flow entirely, which provides a small
  > performance gain compared to parsing at a non-debug logging level.
